description: NIST SRM 612 (trace-element doped glass) LIBS spectra. No publicly accessible experimental LIBS spectra were found after exhausting all ranked sources (PDS SuperCam/ChemCam, MIT DSpace, USGS GeoREM, NIST PML LIBS database, Zenodo, figshare). See README.md for gap analysis and follow-up options.
sources_searched: NASA PDS Mars 2020 SuperCam calibration archive (pds-geosciences.wustl.edu) — SRM variants present: steel SRMs 688, 97a, 98a, 88b; SRM 612 absent
NASA PDS MSL ChemCam calibration archive — no SRM 612
MIT DSpace BHVO-2 thesis search — not found
USGS GeoREM (georem.mpch-mainz.gwdg.de) — composition DB only, no spectra
NIST PML LIBS database (physics.nist.gov/PhysRefData/ASD/LIBS) — atomic line positions only, not measured SRM spectra
Zenodo API search (q=BHVO LIBS, q=SRM 612 LIBS) — no matching datasets
Figshare/Google Scholar — only NIST SRM 610/612 compositional analysis (LA-ICP-MS, not LIBS)
follow_up_options: Request from IRAP SuperCam team (Roger Wiens / Sam Clegg) — they likely have SRM 612 calibration shots not yet deposited in PDS
Check LIBS-ENFL group at University of Malaga — known users of SRM 610/612 for calibration
Check ORDaR platform (ordar.obspm.fr) which hosts LIBS mineral databases from French labs
Synthesize spectra from NIST atomic line database + Boltzmann distribution (requires plasma temperature assumption) — must be flagged as synthetic, not real measurements
